scope.summary: F-LOW DC7: an AUTH_SERIAL_NUM outside u16 was silently wrapped (70000 -> 4464), reporting a session serial the server never issued; it is now rejected through the existing typed session-field error.
out_of_scope: The code itself, which is driver-side (rust-oracledb crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs) and cannot appear in this repo's in_scope; the handoff artifact binds the exact driver commit and file digest instead.
Other session fields parsed elsewhere: only parse_session_u16 carried the wrapping cast.
The three sibling driver-side F-LOW beads (DC5, DC6, DK1, DK2), which remain open.

entry_points.name: parse_session_u16 (rust-oracledb crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs)
entry_points.trace: Driver commit 5ed87b09ef39, pushed to rust-oracledb main and bound by digest in the in-scope handoff artifact. `.parse::<u64>().map(|v| v as u16)` became `.parse::<u16>()`, so an out-of-range value returns Error::MissingSessionField instead of a wrapped number. Negative acceptance: 65536, 70000, 4294967296 and -1 are all refused (auth_serial_num_out_of_u16_range_is_rejected_not_wrapped). Positive acceptance: 0, 1, 4464 and 65535 parse exactly (auth_serial_num_parses_the_full_u16_domain_exactly). 347 oracledb lib tests pass; clippy clean.

limitations: Discriminating, verified by mutation: restoring the `as u16` cast fails the rejection test with 'a serial outside u16 must not be silently wrapped: 0'.

The pre-existing test auth_serial_num_truncates_to_low_u16_instead_of_rejecting was REPLACED, not extended: it asserted 70000 -> 4464 as intended behaviour, which is the defect. The replacement covers strictly more input.
Unit-level. No live Oracle authentication produced an out-of-range AUTH_SERIAL_NUM; the fixtures are constructed session maps.
Cross-repo close: this repo's evidence binds a driver commit by SHA and file digest. The oraclemcp source tree is unchanged by this bead.
